data.title: Gold Prices Plummet Below $3000 Amid Significant Drop
data.description: According to PANews, spot gold prices have experienced a significant decline, falling below the $3,000 per ounce mark. This drop represents a decrease of over $50 from its historical high, with a daily loss of nearly 1.5%.

data.title: BNB Chain has completed the Pascal hard fork, awaiting the Lorentz upgrade in April
data.description:
BNB Chain has completed the Pascal Hardfork, an upgrade designed to boost transaction efficiency and improve its compatibility with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM). Now on to other upgrades scheduled for April and June.  The Pascal Hardfork is a big step forward for the network, making it one of the first to use this important […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bnb-chain-completes-the-pascal-hard-fork/

data.title: Australia Introduces New Crypto Regulation to Address ‘Debanking’ as the Country Faces Possible Recession
data.description:
Just months out from a federal election, the Australian Government has announced a new crypto regulation, aiming to provide more certainty for crypto traders while providing a bit of freedom for blockchain innovators.
source: https://zycrypto.com/australia-introduces-new-crypto-regulation-to-address-debanking-as-the-country-faces-possible-recession/

data.title: Matt Hougan: If stablecoins reach trillions of dollars, the entire crypto market will be reshaped
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan posted on the social platform, quoting US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's speech: "We will ensure that the US dollar continues to be the world's dominant reserve currency, and we will achieve this goal with the help of stablecoins." Hougan said: "No matter how high your expectations for the asset management scale (AUM) of stablecoins are, it is likely that you still underestimate its potential. If the AUM of stablecoins reaches trillions of dollars, the overall landscape of the crypto market will be reshaped."

data.title: U.S. Treasury Aims to Maintain Dollar's Dominance with Stablecoins
data.description: According to Odaily, Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan shared insights on social media, referencing remarks by U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. Bessent emphasized the commitment to ensuring the U.S. dollar remains the leading global reserve currency, with stablecoins playing a crucial role in achieving this objective. Hougan noted that regardless of expectations for the asset management scale of stablecoins, their potential might still be underestimated. He suggested that if stablecoins' assets under management reach trillions of dollars, it could significantly reshape the overall landscape of the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Fed's Williams: The current moderately restrictive policy stance is completely appropriate, and no clear interest rate cut expectations
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: New York Fed President Williams said that given the high uncertainty in monetary policy, the current moderately restrictive stance is "perfectly appropriate" against the backdrop of a solid job market and inflation slightly above the 2% target. He did not elaborate on his specific expectations for this year's policy path in his speech, but pointed out that the U.S. economic foundation was good in early 2025, and although the inflation cooling process was challenging, the job market itself was not a source of price pressure. He added that fiscal, trade policies and geopolitical developments still bring high uncertainty. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Financial Regulators Remove 'Reputation Risk' from Banking Standards
data.description: According to PANews, recent developments indicate that the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) in the United States has officially removed 'reputation risk' from its bank examination criteria. This change follows a commitment by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell last month to eliminate guidelines that instructed staff to monitor bank executives for controversial statements. The OCC's decision marks a shift towards focusing on more transparent risk areas in the future. This move addresses one of the key factors that previously contributed to 'debanking,' where regulatory bodies required banks to consider 'reputation risk' when assessing clients' eligibility for financial services.

data.title: Fed's Williams: Current Monetary Policy Stance Appropriate Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description: According to Odaily, New York Federal Reserve President John Williams stated that the current moderately restrictive monetary policy is "entirely appropriate" given the robust job market and inflation slightly above the 2% target. While he did not specify expectations for this year's policy path, Williams noted that the U.S. economy is fundamentally strong at the beginning of 2025. He acknowledged the challenges in reducing inflation but emphasized that the labor market is not the source of price pressures. Williams also highlighted that fiscal, trade policies, and geopolitical developments continue to contribute to significant uncertainty.
